$comment: Phase 1+2 cumulative; new keys must be added to zh-TW.json in same PR; CI blocks on diff.

metadata.title: FAQ · Wooden Ren Designer
metadata.description: Wooden Ren Designer FAQ: sign-in, subscriptions, payments, invoices, refunds, PWA install, 3D rendering, and other common issues.
metadata.ogDescription: Sign-in, subscriptions, payments, refunds, PWA install, 3D rendering — common questions.

help.introPrefix: Can't find an answer? Contact support:
help.stillStuckBody: Write us with your registered email and a screenshot of the issue — we reply within 3 business days.

account.title: Account & sign-in
account.q1Q: The sign-in button doesn't work when opened from LINE / FB / IG
account.q1A:
LINE / FB / IG in-app browsers don't support Google sign-in. Tap the top-right "⋯" and choose Open in browser, or copy the link to Chrome / Safari first.
Alternatively use Email magic link sign-in: enter your email on the sign-in page and tap the link in your inbox within 10 minutes — no Google account needed.
account.q2Q: The email sign-in link says "link expired"
account.q2A: Email sign-in links are valid for 1 hour. If expired, go back to the sign-in page and request a new one. Don't share the link with anyone — it can only be tapped once and only by you.

billing.title: Subscriptions & payments
billing.q1Q: Does monthly billing auto-renew? How do I cancel?
billing.q1A:
Yes, it auto-renews every 30 days until you cancel. Go to My subscription and tap "Cancel subscription" (you keep access through the end of the current period; no refund on the charge already taken).
Yearly plans do NOT auto-renew. You get a reminder email 7 days before expiry.
billing.q2Q: I paid but my features aren't unlocked yet
billing.q2Intro: Payment callbacks usually complete in 1–5 minutes. If you've waited more than 10 minutes:
billing.q2Step1: Refresh the page / sign out and back in
billing.q2Step2: Check your email for the "Payment successful" notification
billing.q2Step3: Still locked? Contact support with the last 4 digits of your card and the time of the transaction
billing.q3Q: Monthly vs yearly — what's the difference?
billing.q3A: Monthly = auto-renews every 30 days, you cancel to stop. Yearly = pay once for a year, does not auto-renew, comes with a discount. See full pricing on the <linkPricing>pricing page</linkPricing>.
billing.q4Q: Can I upgrade or downgrade mid-cycle?
billing.q4A: Upgrades: yes. Tap your target plan on the <linkPricing>pricing page</linkPricing> and the system auto-prorates the refund (unused days of the old plan go back to your card) and cancels the old recurring charge. Downgrades: wait until the current plan expires, then pick the new one.

invoice.title: Invoices (Taiwan only)
invoice.q1A: After payment succeeds, the system auto-issues an ECPay B2C electronic invoice (Taiwan tax authority format) to your registered email. Configure at <linkSub>My subscription</linkSub> → Invoice preferences.
invoice.q2Q: I entered the wrong company name / tax ID
invoice.q2A:
Within 24 hours: contact support immediately and we void and reissue (automatic).
After 24 hours: requires a manual credit note flow handled by support (1–3 business days).
invoice.q3Q: I didn't receive the invoice email
invoice.q3A: Check spam and the Promotions tab first. If still missing, contact support and we'll manually resend. The invoice itself is already on file with Taiwan's Ministry of Finance e-invoice platform and cannot be lost.

design.title: Designing & using the app
design.q1A: If you weren't signed in when designing, the data only lives in that browser — switching devices or clearing cache wipes it. Sign in first, then save: designs sync to the cloud and appear in My designs across devices.
design.q2Q: 3D model won't load on my phone / freezes
design.q2A: 3D rendering is resource-heavy on older phones / browsers. We recommend desktop + Chrome / Edge or latest iOS Safari. You can also try the "Assembly" view to reduce 3D part count.
design.q3A: Use Chrome → Print → Save as PDF, set margins to "None", and check "Background graphics". Other browsers may break the layout.

pwa.title: Install as desktop app (PWA)
pwa.q1Q: How do I install the app on my phone home screen / computer?
pwa.q1A:
After signing in, the top-right menu shows 📲 Install app to home screen. It may take a few seconds of interaction to appear on the first visit.
iOS Safari: tap the share button ⬆️ at the bottom → Add to Home Screen (must be Safari, not Chrome).
Android / desktop Chrome / Edge: tap the button directly.
Line / FB in-app browsers: not supported, use your system browser.

refundSec.title: Refunds
refundSec.q1Intro: Wooden Ren Designer is a digital virtual product. Per Article 5 of Taiwan's "Reasonable Exceptions to Distance Contract Rescission" regulations, payments are generally non-refundable (you also waived the 7-day cooling-off period at checkout).
refundSec.q1ListIntro: However, the following situations may qualify for case-by-case review:
refundSec.q1Case1: Critical system defect making the service unusable (and we cannot fix it within 7 business days)
refundSec.q1Case2: Service is unilaterally terminated by us, leaving paid-for time unused
refundSec.q1Case3: Other situations required by Taiwan consumer protection law
refundSec.q1Outro: Full terms on the <linkRefund>refund policy page</linkRefund>. To apply: email <linkEmail>support</linkEmail> with subject "Refund Request - <registered email>", and include order ID, payment date, and reason.
refundSec.q2Q: How do I avoid buyer's remorse?
refundSec.q2Intro: Since refunds are restricted, we recommend:
refundSec.q2Tip1: Build 3 furniture pieces on the free tier first to confirm the tool works for you
refundSec.q2Tip2: Not sure between monthly and yearly? Start with monthly at NT$390 for a month, then switch to yearly if you like it
refundSec.q2Tip3: Monthly can be <linkSub>cancelled anytime</linkSub> — access stays through period end

metadata.title: About · Wooden Ren Designer — dimensions in, plans in 3 seconds
metadata.description: From Wooden Ren Academy. 25+ furniture templates. Enter dimensions, get three-view drawings, exploded 3D, joinery details, cut lists, build order, and printable PDFs. Built for DIYers, working woodworkers, designers, and students.
metadata.ogTitle: About · Wooden Ren Designer — dimensions in, plans in 3 seconds
metadata.ogDescription: From Wooden Ren Academy. 25+ templates, auto three-view, joinery, cut list, PDF plans.
metadata.twitterDescription: Dimensions in, plans / cut list / quote in 3 seconds. From Wooden Ren Academy.

hero.intro: Pick a piece, fill in length × width × height, and 3 seconds later you have a 3D view, three-view engineering drawings, joinery details, cut list, build order, and a printable PDF.
hero.triptychCaption: ↑ Same piece, three outputs generated in 3 seconds — 3D view, engineering drawings, cut list

triptych.panel1Alt: 3D view — walnut media console real output

mosaic.intro: Currently 25+ furniture templates — from pencil holders to wardrobes, all resize on the fly with sliders.
mosaic.outro: Just 12 of them shown — <link>see the full catalog</link>

pain.h2: The slow part of woodworking isn't building.
pain.intro: It's drawing the plan right, getting the stock math right, and sequencing the cuts. A stool plan typically eats 2–3 hours. A cabinet, half a day. Burned the afternoon before you even touch wood.

drawing.title: Drawing gets stuck
drawing.desc: Some dimension's always off — the three-view and the perspective just won't add up.

material.title: Stock math gets stuck
material.desc: Length × width × thickness, plus tenon allowance, plus waste, plus planing — half an hour goes counting on fingers.

process.title: Build order gets stuck
process.desc: What goes first, what gets glued, what gets clamped — get it wrong and you can't even take it apart to fix.

pain.conclusion: Designer compresses all three into 3 seconds — the two evenings you save are enough to pick up another commission.

steps.step1Desc: From 25+ furniture templates: stools, dining chairs, desks, wardrobes, wine racks, pencil holders — beginner through advanced.
steps.step2Desc: Length, width, height, stock thickness, joinery type — drag the sliders and watch the 3D update live.
steps.step3Title: Take the plans to the shop
steps.step3Desc: PDF engineering drawings, cut list, tool list, build order — print and start cutting.

outputs.h2: Every piece comes with all of this
outputs.intro: Not just a design — a complete build package, ready for the shop.

threeview.title: 📐 Engineering three-view
threeview.desc: Front, side, top — fully dimensioned, drawn in the conventions a working woodworker recognizes, print-and-build.

cutlist.title: 📋 Cut list
cutlist.desc: L × W × T, part number, 3D location for every piece — check them off as you cut, no mistakes.

cutplan.title: 🪵 Stock layout
cutplan.desc: Most efficient layout auto-computed with utilization percentage. Even tells you which side of the board to start the cut.

process.title: 📑 Build order
process.desc: Full 19-step sequence, time estimates, stock selection notes, moisture content, grain direction — more detailed than the textbook.

quote.title: 💰 Customer quote
quote.desc: Furniture price, tax, deposit / final split, installment terms, customer info table — print it and hand it to the customer.

perspective.title: 🪑 3D perspective preview
perspective.desc: Photoreal wood grain rendering, rotate to any angle — see the finished piece clearly before you cut.

joinery.title: Joinery details
joinery.desc: Through tenons, blind tenons, half-laps — positions auto-computed, with male/female alignment diagrams.

tools.title: Tool list
tools.desc: Which tools this piece needs — beginners don't have to guess what to buy.

stl.title: STL 3D export
stl.desc: Open in SketchUp or your 3D printer. Print a scale model to prototype before you commit.

decor.h2: Measure once, print the whole spec
decor.intro: Interior contractors burn most of their time not at the nail gun — but estimating on site with a pencil, drawing at home, then a second trip for parts. From ceiling frames to storage cabinets: enter the room dimensions, get the full spec.
decor.ceilingDesc: Measure the room and enter L×W×H — main joists, secondary joists, hangers auto-laid out. Length of every member, number of calcium silicate panels, number of nails, total stock and labor — print and bring to the job site.
decor.cabinetsTitle: Cabinet line — {count} common cabinet types, auto plans + cut list

persona.h2: Built for five kinds of people

diy.title: DIY woodworkers
diy.desc: Can't draft engineering drawings but want to build furniture for the home. Pick a template, adjust to your space, let the tool handle the rest.

pro.title: Pro woodworkers / commissions
pro.desc: Customer asks "how much for a cabinet like this?" — design + quote + build order in 5 minutes. Faster than the competition wins the job. Stock math precise so you don't over-order.

interior.title: Interior contractors / renovators
interior.desc: Measure the room and enter L×W×H — ceiling frame (main / secondary / supports) auto-computed, panels, nails, full spec to print and bring on site.

designer.title: Designers
designer.desc: Hand the engineering drawings and cut list straight to the woodworker for fabrication — minimum communication overhead.

student.title: Academy students / self-learners
student.desc: Change the dimensions or swap the joinery on a class project — see the 3D update instantly. Ten times faster than a textbook.

bio.p1: 200,000 people watch me work wood on YouTube. The Wooden Ren Academy, online and in person, has taught 600+ students.
bio.p2: The question I've gotten most often in ten years —
bio.quote: "Hey Ren, how do you figure out this dimension?"
bio.p3: Designer is the tool I built from those answers. Furniture sizing conventions, joinery rules, build-order logic — every single rule comes from work I've actually done and questions I've answered until they're worn smooth.

plans.h2: Try free, upgrade when you need to
plans.intro: Monthly from NT$390, yearly saves two months. One extra commission, or one stock-math mistake avoided — the subscription has already paid itself off.
plans.customTitle: 🪵 Not subscribing? Buy a single template
plans.customBody: Want to build just one chair? Buy that blueprint once and own it forever. No subscription required.
plans.customSeeAll: → See lifetime prices for all 26 templates
plans.perkNoCC: No credit card for free tier
plans.perkRetention: Cancel keeps access until period end
plans.bugReport: Hit a bug or stuck? Tap the Wooden Ren icon at the bottom right of any page (like the one on the left) to report it — it auto-attaches the current page and browser info, and I'll know how to fix it.

metadata.title: Wooden Ren Designer — Plans, cut lists, and joinery in 3 seconds
metadata.description: The slow part of woodworking isn't building — it's drawing the plan right, calculating the stock, and sequencing the cuts. Enter dimensions, pick your wood, and get three-view drawings, exploded 3D, mortise & tenon details, cut lists, and printable PDFs. Free for the square stool and pencil holder; paid plans from $12.9/month.
metadata.ogTitle: Wooden Ren Designer — Plans in 3 seconds
metadata.ogDescription: Drawing, cut list, and quote compressed into 3 seconds. Free for the square stool and pencil holder. Built by the Wooden Ren woodworking academy.

hero.subtitleA: The slow part of woodworking isn't building — it's drawing the plan right, calculating the stock, and sequencing the cuts.
hero.subtitleB: This tool compresses all three into 3 seconds.
hero.note: No signup required to try · Square stool + pencil holder always free
hero.heroAlt: Wooden Ren Designer real output — walnut media console 3D perspective

pain.subtitle: Three frustrations we've heard from woodworkers for three years running.

drawing.title: I can picture it. I can't draw it.
drawing.body: You see it in your head — but how thick should the legs be? How deep the apron? Half an hour in SketchUp and you're still rotating the view.

material.title: Stock math is a nightmare
material.body: Board feet conversions, offcut waste estimation, a fair price that doesn't lose money — same math every single project.

quote.title: Quoting kills the deal
quote.body: A customer texts "how much for a dining table?" and you go home to do the math. Quote it in front of them — that's professional.

outputs.subtitle: Walk into the shop with the cut list and start sawing.

drawings.title: Three-view + joinery details
drawings.body: Front, side, top — auto-dimensioned. Through tenons / blind tenons / staggered half-laps positioned for you.

cutplan.title: Cut list + cutting diagram
cutplan.body: Auto-converted board feet, every piece sized and sequenced for minimum offcut waste.

quote.title: Quote + labor hours
quote.body: Stock × thickness × finishing × labor → total. Pro tier includes customer letterhead and margin.

templates.subtitle: Click any to see what it can do for you — design notes, real output, fit-for-purpose.

scenarios.subtitle: Six real scenarios — which one's most like you?

morning.body: On the couch with your phone, sketch a bedside table — frictionless, light, part of life.

workshop.body: Fresh PDF in hand against the stock — millimeter-precise, fewer rejects.

classroom.body: Every student opens Designer on their phone — abstract becomes concrete, joinery clicks instantly.

customer.body: Customer texts "can you build a dining table?" — pull up the design and iterate in the chat.

midnight.body: Inspiration doesn't wait. Half-asleep design now, refine tomorrow.

kid.body: 30 minutes with your kid designing a pencil holder — woodworking becomes a parent-child tool.

pricing.subtitle: Try free first. Upgrade when it clicks.

free.f4: Size cap: stool 35×35×45 cm, holder 20×20×25 cm

personal.f2: Ceiling frame + flooring + raised platform tools
personal.f4: For DIYers, home reno, weekend furniture builders

pricing.yearNote: Yearly billing saves over a month · Lifetime academy members DM for a code

custom.title: 🪵 Not subscribing? Buy a single template
custom.body: Just want to build one chair? Buy that blueprint once, own it forever. No subscription required.

faq.freeQ: What does the free tier actually include?
faq.freeA: The square stool and pencil holder — full 3D, joinery, three-view, cut list, PDF, exactly what paid users get. The difference is template count and size cap.
faq.cadQ: How does this compare to SketchUp / Fusion 360?
faq.cadA: Designer is for woodworkers who want to build, not learn CAD. Five-minute learning curve, automatic joinery / three-view / cut list / cutting plan. Not trying to replace professional CAD.
faq.tierQ: Personal vs Pro — what's the difference?
faq.tierA: Hobby use: Personal (full 26 templates + reno tools). Earning a living from woodworking: Pro (adds customer quote system, CNC export, unlimited size).
faq.cancelA: Yes. Monthly cancels anytime, keeps access through the current period. Yearly: 7-day no-questions full refund.
faq.cncQ: Can I take the output to CNC?
faq.cncA: Yes. Pro exports STL / OBJ for CNC and laser. Free and Personal three-view PDFs are sufficient for hand tools.

bottomCta.h2Highlight: Then decide if you want to pay.
bottomCta.subtitle: Fairest deal I know — use it first, pay only if it actually helps you.

metadata.description: From square stools, pencil holders and side tables to wardrobes, desks and dining chairs — full overview of 26 parameterizable furniture templates. Each entry shows what it builds, who it suits, what tools you need, and how to vary it.

header.badge: Full template guide · use cases + FAQ

header.subtitle2: Ceiling framing, flooring and tatami platform tools live here too.
header.subtitle3: Read the guide first, then decide whether to build it.

design.wipTemplate: This template is still in development; only the square stool is currently supported.

header.weightTitle: Actual weight = part volume × wood density + 12% packaging

clamp.title: Free-plan size cap reached — auto-clamped
clamp.footer: Need it bigger? Turn on Designer Mode to enter custom mm sizes —

warnings.title: Some parameters had issues (auto-corrected for the render):

section.perspectiveHint: 3D · drag to rotate, scroll to zoom
section.cutListHint: {count} parts · cut sizes include tenon stock
section.cutListNotice: Cut sizes include tenon length. Mortises do not affect outer part dimensions.
section.related: Want to build something similar? Related templates

noJoinery.h: 📐 Mortise-free assembly options (pick one or combine)
noJoinery.row1Body: — vertical board joints (top↔side, shelf↔side). Drill 15° pocket holes with a jig and screw in from the hidden face — no exposed screw heads.
noJoinery.row2Body: — edge-glue panels or reinforce structure with a doweling jig + 8 mm dowels.
noJoinery.row3Body: — closer to true mortise-and-tenon strength using a DOMINO joiner (separate machine required).
noJoinery.row4Body: — easiest for frame work (leg↔apron, stretcher↔leg). Pre-drill pilot holes to avoid splitting.
noJoinery.row5Body: — mechanical fasteners + adhesive is what holds it. Screws and dowels just clamp while the glue cures.
noJoinery.tools: Suggested tools: pocket-hole jig, doweling jig, drill, drill bit set, PVA wood glue, 4× F-clamps, 120/240/400 grit sandpaper.
noJoinery.switchHint: Want traditional mortise-and-tenon (with detail drawings and finer steps)? Toggle Joinery mode on the left.

joinery.noToShow: No joinery to show for this design.

form.modeAssemblyDesc: Screws / dowels / DOMINO / pocket holes. Faster build, ~60–70% strength of joinery — fine for everyday furniture.
form.modeJoineryDesc: Traditional mortise-and-tenon joinery with detail drawings and refined steps. Harder, slower.
form.designerOnDesc: Removes template caps; enter mm-level custom sizes (e.g. 2387 mm wall fit, column-dodge notches — system-cabinet territory).
form.designerOnWarning: ⚠️ Enabled: sizes no longer capped by the template. Extreme sizes may yield unreasonable results (insufficient beam depth, broken joinery) — always verify against the three-view.
form.designerLockedDesc: Removes template size caps; enter mm-level custom sizes (e.g. 2387 mm wall fit, column-dodge notches).
form.autoUpdate: Design auto-updates 0.5s after you change a parameter
form.legReadoutClamped: Clamped to min 30mm — lower the zone heights or raise overall height

jsonLd.howToDesc: Full build steps for a {name} ({length}×{width}×{height} mm, {material}): stock selection, cutting, joinery, assembly, finishing.

topBar.moreTitle: More actions on this page (cut plan / CSV / print)

section.cutListNotice: Cut sizes include tenon stock length
section.joineryFootnote: Full joinery details: ⚙ Advanced → Joinery tab

joinery.noJoinery: No joinery to show for this template.
joinery.switchHint: Switch to “🪵 Joinery” mode to see the joinery details.

advancedSheet.title: ⚙ Advanced
advancedSheet.noStructure: No structure options for this template
advancedSheet.noJoineryOption: No joinery options for this template
advancedSheet.sceneHint: Pick a scene — the 3D view updates background and lighting live

optionField.clampedMin: Clamped to 30 mm min — lower a tier or raise total height

hero.leadLine1: Draw the room → auto-calc battens, fiber-cement boards, hangers →
hero.body: Living room, dining room, hallway — what used to take an afternoon now takes 30 seconds. Main + sub battens auto-align with board seams, hangers distribute evenly, and you can compare 4×8 vs 3×6 board specs to see which wastes less.
hero.footnote: Included in Personal plan — one key unlocks all 26 furniture templates.
hero.shareTitle: Wood Ceiling Framing Simulator | Draw room → calc battens & boards & hangers → A4 quote
hero.diagramLabel1: Main battens + sub battens + fiber-cement board

painSection.h2: The 4 worst things about wood ceiling work
painSection.sub: Exactly what this tool was built to fix

items.title: Adding up batten meters is dizzying
items.body: 12-ft main battens, sub battens criss-crossing one by one, and rooms aren't perfect rectangles. Miss one and you're back at the lumber yard.

items.title: Board seams missing the battens
items.body: Fiber-cement seams float between battens → unsupported edges, cracks by year-end. Aligning them by hand is exhausting.

items.title: Order 4×8 or 3×6 boards?
items.body: Different prices, different waste. Guessing usually overshoots by 20%.

items.title: Quoting means opening Excel again
items.body: Once you finish counting, you still type Excel, paste a logo, format A4. Change a dimension and redo it all — half a day gone.

featureSection.sub: Room shape → batten BOM → quote, end to end

items.title: Room shape: rectangle / L / U / bay window
items.body: Drag length & width, L-shape inner corners, U-shape, pull bay windows out separately. 2D recalc live, 3D structure live.

items.title: Main + sub battens auto-laid
items.body: Main 60×30, sub 30×30, spacing 30/45/60 cm tunable. Auto-tiles across the whole room.

items.title: Auto board layout
items.body: Switch 4×8 vs 3×6 specs side by side. Seams snap to batten centerlines — edges always supported.

items.title: Even hanger distribution
items.body: Hangers auto-laid by room length & width, 60–90 cm range, cross-joints emphasized for 3D legibility.

items.title: 3D 8-layer toggles
items.body: Slab / hangers / main battens / sub battens / boards / joints — toggle each. Clients grasp install order at a glance.

items.title: A4 quote + CSV BOM
items.body: Client info, line items, totals, terms — one A4 page. BOM exports to CSV for the crew.

paramsSection.sub: The algorithm auto-calculates battens, board seams, hanger spread.

items.label: Room shape (rectangle / L / U / bay window)
items.desc: Live 3D as you drag length/width, L/U inner corners tunable, bay windows pulled separately.

items.label: Ceiling height
items.desc: Distance from original slab to wood ceiling — hanger length is auto-calculated.

items.label: Main batten spacing
items.desc: 30 / 45 / 60 cm presets. The system back-solves from board short edge so seams always land on batten centers.

items.label: Sub batten spacing
items.desc: 30 / 45 cm presets. Bigger spans auto-upgrade to heavier battens.

items.label: Fiber-cement board spec (4×8 / 3×6)
items.desc: Toggle to see which spec wastes less — 4×8 means big sheets and low waste; 3×6 ships easier.

items.label: Hanger spacing
items.desc: 60–90 cm range, auto even distribution. The system warns if a span exceeds 90 cm.

items.label: Trim / molding (optional)
items.desc: Add perimeter molding (PS or wood), with door openings auto-deducted from total meters.

items.label: Light-fixture openings (reserved)
items.desc: Mark recessed-light positions and the BOM annotates board cuts so you don't over-cut.

outputsSection.sub: Six artifacts at once — print A4 and bring it to the shop or hand it to the client.

items.title: Batten BOM
items.body: Main and sub batten meters & counts, with 12-ft / 8-ft stock cut plans laid out.

items.title: Board layout drawing
items.body: Compare 4×8 vs 3×6, see which wastes less; 2D layout with auto-numbered panels.

items.title: Hanger spread table
items.body: Hangers auto-laid from room dimensions, spacing within 60–90 cm.

items.title: 3D 8-layer breakdown
items.body: Slab / hangers / main battens / sub battens / boards / joints — toggle each.

items.title: A4 quote
items.body: Client info + line items + totals + terms on one A4 page. Save as PDF from your browser.

items.title: BOM CSV export
items.body: Excel opens cleanly, crews use it for procurement directly.

showcaseSection.sub: These are actual artifacts the tool produces — they update live as you change dimensions.

items.label: 2D plan + main/sub batten grid
items.desc: Drag room dimensions, framing lays out automatically, seams always land on batten centers.

items.label: 3D structure with 8 toggleable layers
items.desc: Slab / hangers / main / sub / boards / joints — clients grasp install order in seconds.

items.label: Cut plan + BOM
items.desc: How each 12-ft batten gets cut, with offcut sizes — hand it to the lumber yard and they cut accordingly.

showcaseSection.footnote: ※ Change dimensions / switch board specs / change batten spacing — these all redraw live.

items.body: Put a 3D view + quote in one proposal, client closes on the spot. Spec changes don't require a redraw.

notForItems: Steel-stud / drywall systems — this tool computes wood framing, not T-bar steel
Non-orthogonal rooms (slanted walls, curves, multiple bays) — v1 supports rectangle, L, and small bay extrusions
Precise positioning of HVAC vents / light openings — this tool sizes framing and boards, not openings
Existing CAD/Revit workflows — this fills the gap for those who lack CAD but still need takeoffs

items.body: Client asks 'how much for the living room ceiling per ping' — pull out your phone, criss-cross framing + board layout + hangers, A4 quote in 30 seconds, close right there.
Pitch ceiling, floor, raised platform together: 3D + quote on one sheet, clients read drawings 10× faster than Excel — no overnight grind.
Tearing down an old drop ceiling, rebuilding wood framing: draw the room → tool tells you how many 12-ft battens and fiber-cement boards to order. Skip asking the contractor.
The framing logic and seam-alignment principles you learned in class — this tool lets you see how the algorithm computes them. Bridge from practice to client work.

items.q: How does wood ceiling framing differ from steel-stud T-bar?
items.a: Wood framing uses 'batten grid + fiber-cement boards' — 12-ft battens with boards screwed on, supports light fixtures, allows shaping. Steel T-bar uses T-bars + mineral fiber tiles, modular and easy to dismantle but weak load and hard to shape. This tool calculates wood framing.

items.q: Should I order 4×8 or 3×6 boards?
items.a: 4×8 (122×244 cm) is the most common in Taiwan — large sheet, good for big living/dining rooms. 3×6 (91×183 cm) is smaller, less waste in small spaces, easier to transport. This tool toggles both specs and compares waste automatically.

items.q: Do seams really auto-align with battens?
items.a: Yes. The system back-solves main batten spacing as 'board short edge ÷ N', so board seams always land on batten centerlines — edges always supported, no year-end cracks. Switch board spec (4×8 / 3×6) and batten spacing auto-updates.

items.q: Can I export PDF / CSV?
items.a: A4 quotes print straight from the browser → Save as PDF. Logo / company info / terms are filled once in 'Branding' panel and reused. BOM exports to CSV, Excel opens cleanly.

items.q: How much does the tool cost?
items.a: Personal NT$ 390/mo — includes all 26 furniture templates + ceiling framing + floor simulator + raised platform. Pro NT$ 890/mo — adds client quoting system + STL/CNC export + unlimited dimensions.

items.q: Can I buy just this tool?
items.a: Yes. Find the single-tool unlock for ceiling on /pricing. But Personal covers all 26 furniture + 3 interior tools — most people end up subscribing to Personal instead.

items.q: What about my bay window or slanted wall?
items.a: v1 supports rectangle, L, and small bay extrusions. Non-orthogonal (slanted walls, curves) are on the roadmap, with seamless upgrade.

items.q: Will the tool keep getting updates?
items.a: Yes. 3D layer toggles, board layout, joint emphasis — all added incrementally after launch. Roadmap: multi-fixture/vent openings, auto load calc, RC slab field data integration.

items.title: Herringbone math fries your brain

items.body: Client asks 'how much more for herringbone in the living room' — switch the pattern on the spot, 2D preview + quote re-runs in one second, client picks pattern right there.
Propose 3 bedrooms + 2 living rooms with different patterns and colors each — all on one A4 page. Spec changes don't require a redraw; 30-minute job done in 5.
Old-house remodel, laying it yourself — draw the room → tool tells you how many laminate planks and skirting strips to order. Walk into the lumber yard ready.
The install logic, start angles, expansion gaps you learned in class — this tool shows you 'how the algorithm computes them'. Bridge from practice to client work.

items.q: What's the difference between laminate, engineered, and solid wood?
items.a: Laminate is HDF with printed wood-grain laminate — durable, scratch-resistant, cheap, but water-damaged. Engineered is HDF topped with 1–2 mm real wood veneer — real-wood feel, moisture-resistant, the mainstream pick. Solid wood is 100% real wood — best feel but most expensive and expands/contracts. This tool supports all three with auto-applied specs and pricing.

items.q: Is herringbone waste really that high?
items.a: Herringbone waste runs 12–22% vs 1.5–6.6% for straight. But herringbone looks expensive and commands premium pricing. This tool pairs herringbone offcuts back (one cut produces two planks shared between bays), shows you actual waste, and the lumber yard's blanket 20% markup can save you 5–10%.

items.q: What does center-start mean? Should I choose it?
items.a: Center-start = laying from room centerline outward, so first/last rows have symmetric width. Pros: symmetric edge waste, most visually balanced. Cons: one seam down the middle. For most living rooms we recommend center-start; small rooms can stay top-left.

items.q: How is this different from 'Tatami Raised Platform'?
items.a: The floor simulator computes flat-laid wood flooring (laminate / engineered / solid) with no frame — directly on the slab. The raised platform is a 30 cm+ podium with top frame, bottom frame, main joists, sub joists, plywood, underlay, skirting. Both share the same unlock key — buy one, unlock both.

items.q: Can I export PDF / CSV?
items.a: A4 quotes print straight from the browser → Save as PDF. Logo / company info / terms are filled once in 'Branding' panel and reused. BOM exports to CSV, Excel opens cleanly.

items.q: How much does the tool cost?
items.a: Personal NT$ 390/mo — includes all 26 furniture templates + ceiling framing + floor simulator + raised platform. Pro NT$ 890/mo — adds client quoting system + STL/CNC export + unlimited dimensions.

items.q: Can I buy just the floor simulator?
items.a: Yes. The floor simulator shares the 'floor' unlock key with the Tatami Raised Platform, so a single-tool purchase unlocks both. See /pricing.

items.q: My place isn't a perfect rectangle — anything besides L?
items.a: v1 supports rectangle, L, T, U, cross, Z, hexagon — 7 orthogonal polygons, plus custom polygons. Slanted walls and curves not yet supported but on the roadmap.

items.q: Will the tool keep getting updates?
items.a: Yes. Herringbone algorithm, 9 room shapes, 5 start angles, 2D offcut pairing — all added incrementally after launch. Roadmap: slanted walls / curves, 3D perspective, thermal expansion tips, vendor spec database integration.

painSection.h2: The 4 worst things about tatami / raised-platform takeoffs
painSection.sub: Exactly what this tool was built to fix

items.title: Brain-fryer math, easy to over- or under-order
items.body: How many main joists, sub joists, plywood sheets, underlay rolls — each in different units and different logic. A pen and paper for 30 minutes might still come out wrong.

items.title: Plywood seams not landing on joists
items.body: Set main/sub spacing wrong and plywood seams float between joists — you'll have to add extra battens to support them, wasting material and time.

items.title: Order too much plywood, leftovers pile up in storage
items.body: 11 offcut pieces could pack into 4 new sheets, but without 2D packing math people just multiply 'pieces × 1.2 waste' → 30% over-order.

items.title: Client quotes still need Excel formatting
items.body: Once you finish counting, you still type Excel, paste a logo, format A4 — half a day per quote. Change one dimension and redo it all.

items.body: Client asks 'how much per ping for a 3 m × 4 m tatami' — pull out your phone, sketch 3D and A4 quote in 30 seconds, close right there. Spec changes recompute instantly; no Excel back at the studio.

Building a sleeping platform, a guest tatami room, a storage podium — draw the platform → tool tells you how many 12-ft battens, how many plywood sheets, how many underlay rolls. Walk into the lumber yard ready.

items.q: How is this different from the Floor Simulator?
items.a: The floor simulator computes flat-laid wood flooring (laminate / engineered) with no frame. The raised platform is a 30 cm+ podium with top frame, bottom frame, main joists, sub joists, plywood, underlay, skirting — the wood-construction method for tatami / underlay beds / guest sleeping platforms. Both share the same unlock key — buy one, unlock both.

items.q: Does the plywood packing really save material?
items.a: Real test: 11 cut pieces packed via 2D guillotine — a 3 m × 4 m tatami originally costing '8 full sheets + 11 cuts = 19 plywood sheets' becomes '8 full sheets + 4 new sheets cut into 11 pieces' = 12 sheets total. Saves 7 sheets (~35%), NT$ 3,500+ saved.

items.q: Is the quote accurate?
items.a: Material quantity is BOM-precise (main joist meters, plywood sheet counts, underlay rolls — units all correct). Unit prices use your own lumber-yard cost and your own labor rate. The system doesn't sneak in 'market price' inflation — all unit-price fields are blank for you to fill. Whatever you fill is what gets calculated.

items.q: Can I export PDF / CSV?
items.a: A4 quotes print straight from the browser → Save as PDF. Logo / company info / terms are filled once in 'Branding' panel and reused. BOM exports to CSV, Excel opens cleanly.

items.q: How much does the tool cost?
items.a: Personal NT$ 390/mo — includes all 26 furniture templates + ceiling framing + floor simulator + raised platform. Pro NT$ 890/mo — adds client quoting system + STL/CNC export + unlimited dimensions.

items.q: Can I buy just the raised platform tool?
items.a: Yes. The raised platform shares the 'floor' unlock key with the Floor Simulator — a single-tool purchase unlocks both. See /pricing.

items.q: My place isn't a perfect rectangle — anything besides L?
items.a: v1 supports rectangle, L-shape, and up to 2 column notches. Non-orthogonal polygons (slanted walls, U-shape, cross shape) are on the roadmap and will land in the same wave as the floor simulator — seamless upgrade.

items.q: Will the tool keep getting updates?
items.a: Yes. The tool launched 2026-05 — 3D layer toggles, plywood packing algorithm, install-step cards, underlay catalog were all added within 2 weeks of launch. Roadmap: more columns, slanted edges, herringbone surfaces, on-site cutouts (light fixtures / outlets).

designLoading.body: First load takes a few seconds, please wait.

designError.h1: These design parameters caused a render failure
designError.body: Usually a dimension combination outside the reasonable range. You can reset to defaults, or report this bug to Wooden Ren.

quote.weightTitle: Actual weight = parts volume × density + 12% packaging
quote.lowHoursWarn: Labor unusually low ({hours}h) — template estimate may be off; consider manual override
quote.designerMarkupHint: Added on top of maker price {price}
quote.exclusionHardware: Hardware: slides, hinges, pulls, locks (added separately if needed)
quote.exclusionChange: Post-order changes to design/dimensions/material: re-quoted with change fees
quote.brandingNote: 🏢 Company info, payments, terms moved to the settings page — set once, reuse everywhere
quote.helpH: ℹ️ Quote notes (material units, labor source)
quote.helpItem1: Materials in board-feet (1 bf = 1" × 12" × 12" ≈ 2,360 cm³ ≈ 8.5 Taiwanese tsai); includes cut waste (small parts 25%, normal 10%)
quote.helpItem2: Labor auto-summed from build steps and chamfer ops; override the total in the form for special requirements
quote.helpItem3: One workday = 6 effective hours (excludes communication, cleanup, tool changes, breaks)
quote.helpItem4: Tool estimates are reference only; final quote per maker's on-site conditions

print.previewHint: A4 quote preview — click the button, then choose 'Save as PDF' in the system dialog
print.designCaption: Three-view shows dimensions and proportions; actual wood grain and color vary with natural wood.
print.termShippingExcluded: Shipping not included; quoted by actual delivery address.
print.termInstallExcluded: On-site install not included; quoted separately on request.

roomSize.hint: Drag the slider; preview and BOM recompute live

params.subtitle: Change anything — preview and BOM update live
params.frameDoublesAsSupport: Frame doubles as support (−2 main joists)
params.jointGapHint: Industry 3-6 mm; 9 mm board uses 3 mm
params.autoSpacingLockHelp: Locked: auto-computed from board spec. Uncheck above to drag manually.
params.hangerMinimalHelp: N/A in minimal mode (each main joist has only one hanger at each end)

bom.subtitle: {count} items total · click a row to highlight in the preview

tips.jointDesc: Default {gap} mm; epoxy filler + 48 mm tape + skim coat
tips.alignDesc: Interior board edges land on main joist centerlines (blue ticks); perimeter edges land on frame outer line
tips.screwDesc: ≥ 15 mm from edge; every 20-30 cm along the edge

fixtures.emptyHint: Click a button above to add an opening. X (along long edge) / Z (along short edge), measured from top-left (0, 0) in cm.
fixtures.collisionFix: Fix: move fixtures / change dimensions / change main-joist alignment to dodge them.

cutting.spliceHint: (pieces longer than stock auto-split into segments with overlap)
cutting.splicedTitle: 🔗 Pieces split for splicing ({groups} parts → {pieces} segments, {overlap} cm overlap per joint)
cutting.splicedFooter: Practice: place splice joints on the frame for rigidity (or reinforce with metal plates).

steps.subtitle: 5-step process
steps.step1Title: Frame the perimeter at target height
steps.step1Desc: Wall edge as datum; battens grow inward
steps.step2Title: Main joists span the short edge
steps.step2Desc: Main spacing matches fiber-cement board width
steps.step4Desc: Use ceiling finish as datum; fix hangers
steps.step5Desc: Interior edges land on main centerlines; perimeter edges on frame outer line

customer.footnotePart1: Customer info is only used for PDF print + share-link confirmation. For logo / company info, see

trace.label: 🔬 Formula trace
trace.rowMainCenters: Main joist center positions (along long edge, cm)
trace.rowMainLenCount: Main joist unit length × actual count
trace.rowSubYOffsets: Sub joist Y offsets (from innerY0, cm)

footerDisclaimer.title: ⚠ Usage notice
footerDisclaimer.line1Suffix: . Adjust per on-site conditions (slab levels, beams/columns, MEP routing).
footerDisclaimer.line2Suffix: to cover on-site cut waste, miscount, and defective sheets.
footerDisclaimer.line3Prefix: For structural load, fire, water, and acoustic evaluation, consult certified
footerDisclaimer.line4Suffix: for losses arising from the use of this tool's output.

floorTool.subtitle: Laminate / engineered wood floors · straight & herringbone layout takeoffs and pricing
floorTool.canvasHint: Drag the slider or type to set the outline; notches scale proportionally. Drag corner points to fine-tune, or click the edge number to set that edge length.
floorTool.selfIntersectWarning: ⚠ Room outline self-intersects — takeoffs and preview will be inaccurate. Adjust the shape.
floorTool.traceLine: Full {full} + cut {cut} pieces (offcut-optimized → {newSheets} new sheets) = {total} planks · {rows} rows · waste {waste}%

client.subtitle: Full takeoff for frame + surface · rectangle / L + 0–2 column notches
client.pillarEmptyHint: No columns; click + to add and pick position and notch size.
client.mainJoistLabel: Main joist batten (top + bottom frame shared)
client.plywoodSpecHint: 💡 Pick a preset for standard sizes; fine-tune ±1cm to match your lumber yard.
client.csvTitle: Includes BOM + 3 cut plans (UTF-8 BOM, Excel-safe)
client.splicedTitle: 🔗 Pieces split for splicing ({groups} parts → {pieces} segments, {overlap} cm overlap per joint)
client.plankCutSummary: Full {full} + cut {cut} (new pieces used {newPieces}) = {total} planks · waste {waste}%
client.plywoodSavedBanner: ♻️ 2D offcut packing saves {n} new sheets (platform needs {laid} pieces but order only {ordered} sheets of {longCm}×{shortCm}cm; smaller pieces cut from full-sheet leftovers)
client.newSheetGroupTitle: 📦 New-sheet cut grouping (same number = same sheet)
client.plywoodFooter: 💡 Seams auto-snap to nearest main/sub joist center (every piece edge is nailed to framing). Full-sheet sizing {longCm}cm × {shortCm}cm ± 15% counts as 'full-sheet cut'.
client.step1Desc: Snap a line on the bare slab to mark the platform outline (columns / L-shape per drawing), then anchor the bottom frame batten along the outline; notch inward at column corners and leave the column body uncovered.
client.step2Desc: Perimeter legs stand on the bottom frame; interior legs stand on the bottom main joist (all use {joist}); align the {rows} rows of interior legs to platform level with adjustable feet, lock tight.
client.step3Desc: Anchor the top frame along the platform outline; {joist} main joists span the short axis at {spacing}cm centers, {rows} total; top faces co-linear, laser level the whole platform.
client.step4Desc: {joist} sub joists span between mains, {spacing}cm spacing, {count} total; every plywood edge must hit a sub joist so it doesn't feel soft underfoot.
client.step5Desc: {full} full + {cut} cut pieces; nail seams over main/sub centers, leave {gap}mm gaps between pieces (for moisture expansion; no AB glue).
client.step6Desc: {lengthCm}×{widthCm}cm planks along long axis with 1/2 stagger; leave {gap}mm expansion gap at walls; skirting / edge trim goes on last.
client.copyPlatformLine: Platform {area} m² / {ping} ping · perimeter {perimeter} m · height {height} cm

underlaySkirting.underlayPriceHint: Default price NT$ {price}/roll (overridable in pricing section)
underlaySkirting.skirtingFormulaHint: Skirting length = perimeter − door count × door width
